event_blocks.summary: At the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena, a women's doubles table tennis match unfolds between RYU / KIM (South Korea) in light blue and black, and WANG / SUN (China) in red, with the Chinese pair initially leading 2-0 in games. The distinct sounds of the ball hitting the table and rackets, along with the umpire's calls like "One!", "Two!", and "Three!", punctuate the fast-paced rallies. Commentators analyze the effectiveness of down-the-line shots and the strategic advantage of South Korea's left-hand/right-hand combination against China's two right-handed players. WANG / SUN demonstrate strong offensive play with powerful forehand drives and quick footwork, forcing RYU / KIM into defensive blocks and sharp returns, though commentators note Korea is starting to find a better rhythm and deal better with China's top spin. The audience, visible in the dimly lit arena with blue and red accent lighting, cheers, with some holding '孙颖莎' signs and chanting "Jiayou!" for the Chinese team. The court is branded with logos such as 'WTT', 'JOOLA', 'GantOn', and 'GAME ON', and the venue name 'MAVERICK ARENA' is displayed. Despite WANG / SUN's lead, which progresses through scores like 6-3, 4-7, 8-4, 9-6, and 10-7, RYU / KIM secures points, including a beautiful backhand and a point won by Korea, and the scoreboard updates to 0-2 for RYU / KIM after a point won by WANG / SUN. The commentators discuss the umpire's warnings about server toss adjustments and praise specific shots like a 'good cut' and a 'great flick' by Sun Yingsha, noting her 'real power.' The match sees intense rallies, including a dramatic dive by a Chinese player, and WANG / SUN ultimately win the first game 11-7, leading 1-0 in games. The second game begins with RYU / KIM trailing 0-1 in games and 0-1 in points, but they win a point, and the scoreboard updates to 0-2 for RYU / KIM, indicating a point won by WANG / SUN, with commentators remarking on the Chinese pair's confidence being affected after some lost points.

Umpire.description: A male official seated at the center of the court, wearing a dark shirt, overseeing the match with a neutral posture, occasionally gesturing to signal points.

RYU / KIM (South Korea).description: Two female players in light blue and black uniforms; one with a ponytail and the other with short hair, both displaying focused expressions and agile movements during rallies, with visible sponsor logos like 'POSCO' and a cartoon tiger on their shirts. Commentators highlight a 'beautiful backhand' leading to a 'point won by Team Korea' and note 'Team Korea' is 'dealing better with the top spin shots of Team China'.

Audience.description: Spectators seated in the background, visible in dim lighting, reacting to the match with visible engagement, some leaning forward during intense rallies, and holding signs such as one with '孙颖莎' in bold yellow characters. They are addressed by an announcement requesting 'No flash photography please. Thank you.'

WANG / SUN (China).description: Two female players in red uniforms with yellow trim and 'CHINA' on the back; one with short black hair and the other with short brown hair, both showing aggressive play and strong coordination, with visible 'LI-NING' and 'CHINA' branding on their shirts. Commentators identify one player as 'Sun Yingsha' after an 'unusual miss hit' and discuss the 'confidence affected for the Chinese pair after those losses'.

Maverick Arena.description: The venue in Las Vegas, featuring a dark court with illuminated branding, including 'WELCOME TO THE MAVERICK ARENA', 'LAS VEGAS', and sponsor logos like JOOLA, GantOn, and WTT, with a large screen displaying the Statue of Liberty and cityscape during a transition.

raw_segments: The video opens with a wide-angle view of a high-energy women's doubles table tennis match at the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena, featuring RYU / KIM (South Korea) in light blue and black, and WANG / SUN (China) in red. The scoreboard shows WANG / SUN leading 2-0 in games, with the current game score progressing from 0-0 to 0-3 as the Chinese team scores, punctuated by the distinct sound of the ball hitting the table and rackets, and a clear male voice announcing "One!", "Two!", and "Three!" for each point. The audience, visible in the dimly lit arena with blue and red accent lighting, responds with applause and cheering. Commentators analyze the play, with one noting, "When you can get a down the line shot with this kind of speed against your opponent, it's so effective." The Chinese team demonstrates strong offensive play with powerful forehand drives and quick footwork, while the Korean team responds with defensive blocks and sharp returns. Close-up shots highlight the intense focus of players like the Chinese player with short black hair and the Korean player with a ponytail. Another commentator, addressed as "Ruback," observes the advantage of the Korean team's left-hand/right-hand combination, leading to a discussion about how two right-handed players can "get tangled up amongst each other a lot more than a righty-lefty combo." Dynamic transitions, including a red and blue graphic split-screen, emphasize the match's intensity. The segment concludes with the Chinese team celebrating a point, and the score being called as "Three, two!", followed by more audience applause, as the Korean team regroups.

The minute captures a fast-paced women's doubles table tennis match at the United States Smash 2025, held in the Maverick Arena, Las Vegas. The match features RYU / KIM (South Korea) in light blue and black uniforms against WANG / SUN (China) in red uniforms with yellow trim. Commentary highlights the challenge of needing to "tangle up Wang and Sun" as the Chinese pair dominates the rally with aggressive forehand drives and quick footwork, forcing the South Korean team into defensive positions. The umpire's clear voice announces the scores, calling out "Two-four," "Two-five," and later "Five-three." The audience, visible in the dimly lit arena, responds with enthusiastic cheers and distinct chants of "Jiayou!" for the Chinese team. The scoreboard shows WANG / SUN leading, with the overall match score at 2-0 in their favor. Commentary also discusses how the umpire's warnings about a server's toss often lead players to adjust to a "higher toss." The sequence includes dynamic camera angles—close-ups of players’ intense focus and wide shots of the court, which is branded with logos like JOOLA and WTT, alongside arena displays like 'WELCOME TO THE MAVERICK ARENA'. The rally concludes with a point for WANG / SUN, who celebrate briefly, while the resilient South Korean team maintains focus and prepares for the next serve despite the deficit.

The high-intensity women's doubles table tennis match at the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena unfolds, featuring the Chinese pair WANG / SUN in red uniforms against South Korea's RYU / KIM in light blue and black. The segment opens with the distinct sounds of a table tennis rally, followed by the umpire's clear call of '6-3.' The audience responds with applause and murmurs as the Chinese team, identified by their 'CHINA' and 'LI-NING' branding, continues to dominate with aggressive forehand drives and precise coordination. A female commentator praises a player's ability to 'pick that one up,' noting it 'looked a little bit iffy,' while a male commentator analyzes a 'good cut' shot. Another rally ensues, punctuated by the umpire announcing '4-7,' confirming WANG / SUN's lead, which visually progresses from 7-4 to 8-4. The male commentator exclaims, 'Oh, that's a great flick there by you,' describing how a player 'got over that table and just flat hit that thing,' though regretting not seeing the 'flick' on replay. The intense rallies showcase the players' athleticism and strategic play. The female commentator specifically highlights 'real power from Sun Yingsha,' one of the Chinese players, whose name is also visible on an audience sign, adding that 'this Chinese pair... both bring something really different to the table.' The court is dynamically lit, with 'WTT', 'JOOLA', 'GantOn', and 'GAME ON' branding visible, and a brief transition graphic displays the Statue of Liberty and Las Vegas skyline, emphasizing the event's location. The audience remains focused, some holding signs supporting the Chinese players.

The video opens with a dynamic transition graphic showcasing the Statue of Liberty and Las Vegas skyline, announcing the United States Smash 2025 at the Maverick Arena. The action immediately cuts to a high-intensity women's doubles table tennis match, where RYU / KIM of South Korea, in light blue and black uniforms, face WANG / SUN of China, clad in red. Two commentators, one of whom is addressed as Rebecca, provide analysis, praising the Chinese pair's complementary styles and referring to them as "number one and number two" players. WANG / SUN dominate the early rallies with aggressive forehand drives and precise coordination, while RYU / KIM respond with quick footwork and defensive blocks. The scoreboard reflects WANG / SUN's lead, moving from 8-4 to 9-6, with the umpire's clear voice calling out "6-8" at one point. The commentators note that while Korea has had "some success with the cuts," Wang and Sun's "spin" and "top spin" are causing them "a lot of problems." The court is brightly lit with 'WTT' and 'GantOn' branding, as the dimly lit audience, some holding '孙颖莎' signs, watches intently, their applause and general crowd noise punctuating the fast-paced rallies and distinct ball sounds. The Chinese pair continues to extend their lead, showcasing superior control and teamwork, culminating in a sharp cross-court shot that secures another point.

At the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena, the high-intensity women's doubles table tennis match sees the Chinese pair WANG / SUN, in red 'CHINA' and 'LI-NING' uniforms, leading 9-7 against South Korea's RYU / KIM, dressed in light blue and black with 'POSCO' and a cartoon tiger logo. Commentators note that one player, 'Yung, did really well' in a rally, maneuvering her body and footwork, and observe that 'Korea [is] starting to get into a better rhythm in this game,' leading to 'more rallies now.' Close-up shots reveal the focused expressions of both teams amidst quick exchanges, aggressive forehand drives, and precise footwork. The umpire, seated centrally, observes as the score updates to 10-7, a game point for WANG / SUN, prompting visible excitement and cheers from the audience, some holding '孙颖莎' signs. Commentators describe a 'good serve' and 'nice finish' for this point. The rally intensifies with strong coordination from the Chinese players, one executing a powerful backhand while the other covers the net. The crowd erupts as WANG / SUN win the game 11-7, with commentators announcing 'Game to Sun Wang.' The scoreboard updates to 1-0 in games for WANG / SUN. The arena's dark court is illuminated by vibrant 'LAS VEGAS', 'WTT', 'JOOLA', and 'GantOn' branding, with a large screen briefly showing a stylized cityscape and the Statue of Liberty. Commentators praise 'The Chinese pair' for their 'really good success with their cuts in that short game,' highlighting 'this shot right here by Sun' as the match continues with renewed energy for the second game, showcasing the athletes' competitive spirit under dynamic lighting.

The video segment opens with a close-up of a RYU / KIM player from South Korea, dressed in a light blue and black uniform, bending over the table, before transitioning to a wide-angle view of the doubles match in progress at the Maverick Arena in Las Vegas. The scoreboard shows RYU / KIM trailing 0-1 in games and 0-1 in points against WANG / SUN from China, who are in red uniforms with yellow trim. The rally begins with WANG / SUN serving, and the players engage in a fast-paced exchange, showcasing quick footwork and precise shots. A commentator exclaims, "Oh, what a beautiful backhand and a point won by Team Korea!" The camera alternates between wide shots capturing the full court and close-ups highlighting the players' focused expressions and athletic movements. Following a point, the Chinese pair celebrates with a high-five, while the Korean pair regroups, and the scoreboard updates to 0-2 for RYU / KIM, indicating a point won by WANG / SUN. An announcement is heard, "No flash photography please. Thank you." The match continues with intense rallies, including a dramatic dive by one of the Chinese players to return a shot. A commentator notes, "Well, that was an unusual miss hit from Sun Yingsha," and further observes that "Team Korea [is] dealing better with the top spin shots of Team China, really covering the ball, starting to put them on the table and extend the rally." The audience remains engaged, with some spectators leaning forward during key moments, holding signs such as one for '孙颖莎'. The court is branded with logos like 'WTT', 'GantOn', 'JOOLA', and 'GAME ON', and the venue’s name 'MAVERICK ARENA' is prominently displayed, all under dynamic red and blue lighting. The umpire remains seated at the center, observing the match. The video concludes with a close-up of the Chinese players, one with short black hair and the other with short brown hair, both displaying determination and coordination, as the scoreboard reflects the ongoing game progression, with a commentator remarking on the "confidence affected for the Chinese pair after those losses."

event_blocks.summary: At the WTT United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena, WANG / SUN (China), clad in red, aggressively compete against RYU / KIM (South Korea), who are in light blue and black, in a women's doubles table tennis match, marked by sharp forehand drives and precise returns, with the distinct sound of paddle strikes audible as the commentators praise the players' resilience and past achievements, including a 'big WTT final together' in Nagoya in 2023; the scoreboard indicates WANG / SUN's lead at 2-0 in games and 4-0 in the current game, with the umpire calling out 'Two-four!' after a point, while the audience, some holding '孙颖莎' signs, watches intently. The scene transitions to an Allstate Commercial depicting a marina, followed by a 'Douluo Continent Game Advertisement' featuring a character in a white dress running through a steampunk cityscape with floating structures, accompanied by dramatic, futuristic music and Chinese voiceovers stating '好玩不累' and '重返大陆', concluding with a download prompt.

Douluo Continent Game Advertisement.description: A fantasy-themed video game ad showcasing a character in a white dress running through a steampunk city with floating structures, glowing elements, and a large clock-like gate, with Chinese text '斗罗大陆' and a download link displayed. The ad is accompanied by dramatic, futuristic music and Chinese voiceovers stating "好玩不累" (Fun and not tiring) and "重返大陆" (Return to the Continent).

RYU / KIM (South Korea).description: Two female players in light blue and black uniforms, one with a ponytail and the other with short hair, both displaying focused expressions and agile movements during rallies, with visible sponsor logos like 'POSCO' and a cartoon tiger on their shirts. Commentators describe them as 'warriors' who 'shake it off and play like professionals,' known for their 'big titles,' and a female commentator recalls a 'big WTT final together' in Nagoya in 2023. The sound of the ball hitting the paddle is heard during their play.

Audience.description: Spectators seated in the background, visible in dim lighting, reacting to the match with visible engagement, some leaning forward during intense rallies, and holding signs such as one with '孙颖莎' (Sun Yingsha) in bold yellow characters.

WANG / SUN (China).description: Two female players in red uniforms with yellow trim and 'CHINA' on the back, one with short black hair and the other with short brown hair, both showing aggressive play and strong coordination, with visible 'LI-NING' and 'CHINA' branding on their shirts. Commentators describe them as 'warriors' who 'shake it off and play like professionals,' known for their 'big titles,' and a female commentator recalls a 'big WTT final together' in Nagoya in 2023. The sound of the ball hitting the paddle is heard during their play.

Maverick Arena.description: The venue in Las Vegas, featuring a dark court with illuminated branding, including 'WELCOME TO THE MAVERICK ARENA', 'LAS VEGAS', and sponsor logos like JOOLA, GantOn, and WTT, with a large screen displaying the Statue of Liberty and cityscape during a transition.

Umpire.description: A male official seated at the center of the court, wearing a dark shirt, overseeing the match with a neutral posture, who explicitly calls out "Two-four!" to signal the score.

Allstate Commercial.description: A promotional segment featuring a marina with docked boats, overcast skies, and text overlays such as 'It’s also smart to check Allstate first, for a quote that could save you hundreds.' and the slogan 'you're in good hands'.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a high-intensity women's doubles table tennis match at the WTT United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena. Male commentators discuss the players, noting their "warrior" spirit and ability to "shake it off and play like professionals," despite sometimes losing more than they win, yet being remembered for their "big titles." The Chinese pair WANG / SUN, in red uniforms, aggressively play against the South Korean pair RYU / KIM, in light blue and black, executing sharp forehand drives and precise returns, accompanied by the distinct sound of the ball hitting the paddle. The scoreboard shows WANG / SUN leading 2-0 in games and 4-0 in the current game. The umpire, seated centrally, calls out "Two-four!" after a point, while the audience, some holding '孙颖莎' signs, watches intently. A female commentator adds historical context, recalling a "big WTT final together" in Nagoya in 2023. The scene then abruptly shifts to an Allstate commercial showing a marina, followed by a fantasy game advertisement for '斗罗大陆' (Douluo Continent). This ad features a character in a white dress running through a steampunk cityscape with floating structures, accompanied by dramatic, futuristic music and Chinese voiceovers stating "好玩不累" (Fun and not tiring) and "重返大陆" (Return to the Continent), ending with a download prompt.

event_blocks.summary: The United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena hosted a high-intensity women's doubles table tennis match between China's WANG / SUN and South Korea's RYU / KIM. Initially, a fantasy-themed advertisement for the 'Douluo Continent' game played, featuring a female protagonist in a steampunk city and a library, culminating in a combat sequence with epic orchestral music and Chinese narration, followed by a promotional screen. The live match then commenced, with WANG / SUN in red uniforms facing RYU / KIM in light blue and black. Commentators noted WANG / SUN's past success and highlighted their aggressive play, with one praising a "great backhand" and "real power from the forehand." The score progressed through various stages, with WANG / SUN often holding a lead, such as 4-0 in one game and 6-0 in another, though the match remained tightly contested with scores reaching 7-7, 7-6, 7-5, 7-6, and 7-all. The umpire audibly called scores like "2-5," "Seven-five," "Seven-six," "Seven all," and "Time out," while also enforcing rules against flash photography. Players exhibited focused expressions and agile movements, with close-ups highlighting their technique amidst the distinct sounds of the ball hitting paddles and the table. The audience, visible in the dimly lit arena with prominent 'WTT' and 'LAS VEGAS' branding, reacted with visible engagement, cheers, roars, and applause, some holding '孙颖莎' signs and chanting '加油' (Jiāyóu - Go!) in Chinese. Commentators discussed the Korean pair's quickness and praised specific plays, such as a "nice cut" by "Sun" and a "nicely done" point by "Kim." The match saw WANG / SUN secure game points and eventually win the second game with a "cut from Sun," leading to celebrations like fist bumps. The overall match score fluctuated, with WANG / SUN leading 1-0 in games, then 2-1, and later 2-0 in games with a 4-1 lead in the current game. The intensity was palpable, with commentators noting China's "lockdown mode" and Korea's "near misses." The environment was enhanced by illuminated sponsor logos and large screens displaying graphics like the Statue of Liberty. The segment concluded with transitions to commercials for Allstate and the 'Douluo Continent' game.

Douluo Continent Game Advertisement.description: A fantasy-themed video game ad showcasing a character in a white dress running through a steampunk city with floating structures, glowing elements, and a large clock-like gate, with Chinese text '斗罗大陆' and a download link displayed.

RYU / KIM (South Korea).description: Two female players in light blue and black uniforms with 'POSCO' and a cartoon tiger logo; one with a ponytail and the other with short hair. The short-haired player, identified as Kim, shows visible frustration after losing a point but also executes a powerful backhand. Both display focused expressions and agile movements during rallies.

Audience.description: Spectators seated in the background, visible in dim lighting, reacting to the match with visible engagement, including loud cheers and a "Jia you!" chant. Some hold signs, such as one with '孙颖莎' (Sun Yingsha) in bold yellow characters, and commentators note their enthusiasm for Sun Yingsha and Wang Manyu.

WANG / SUN (China).description: Two female players, Sun Yingsha and Wang Manyu, in red uniforms with yellow trim and 'CHINA' on the back, also featuring 'LI-NING' branding. One has short black hair and the other short brown hair. They display intense focus, aggressive play, strong coordination, and quick footwork, celebrating points with a fist bump. Commentators note their popularity and Wang's skill.

Maverick Arena.description: The Maverick Arena in Las Vegas, featuring a dark court with illuminated branding including 'WELCOME TO THE MAVERICK ARENA', 'LAS VEGAS', and sponsor logos like JOOLA, GantOn, and WTT. The court floor has 'GAME ON' and 'WTT' logos, and the surrounding barriers display multiple sponsor names. The arena is filled with many enthusiastic fans, as noted by commentators.

Umpire.description: A male official seated at the center of the court, wearing a dark shirt, overseeing the match with a neutral posture, occasionally gesturing to signal points. He remains seated and attentive throughout the rally.

Allstate Commercial.description: A promotional segment featuring a marina with docked boats, overcast skies, and text overlays such as 'It’s also smart to check Allstate first, for a quote that could save you hundreds.' and the slogan 'you're in good hands'.

raw_segments: The video opens with a fantasy-themed game advertisement for 'Douluo Continent,' featuring a female character in a white dress running through a steampunk-style city towards a glowing clock gate. Mechanical whirring and whooshing sounds accompany her as she enters a dimly lit library filled with bookshelves and gears, where she confronts armored characters, including a central figure in ornate purple armor. Dramatic, epic orchestral music with Chinese narration and magical battle sound effects, including whooshes and impacts, underscore a dynamic combat sequence. The protagonist and allies then run toward a swirling portal in a snowy, icy chamber, as the music swells with a female choir singing in Chinese. The scene transitions to a promotional screen displaying the game's title '斗罗大陆' with multi-device compatibility and download links, accompanied by English text describing the game’s world. The video then cuts to a live table tennis match at the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas. WANG / SUN (China), in red uniforms, are seen in an intense rally against RYU / KIM (South Korea), in light blue and black uniforms, with the distinct sounds of the ball hitting paddles and the table. A male commentator notes WANG / SUN's past success at the World Championships in Houston in 2021. The scoreboard shows WANG / SUN leading 4-0 in the current game, 2-1 in the match, with the umpire centrally seated. Following a rally, an audible 'Oh!' from the crowd precedes the umpire clearly calling out the score '2-5'. The audience, visible in the dimly lit Maverick Arena with prominent 'WTT' and 'LAS VEGAS' branding, reacts with visible engagement, some holding '孙颖莎' signs, and enthusiastically chants '加油' (Jiāyóu - Go!) in Chinese, supporting the China team. Players exhibit focused expressions and agile movements, with close-ups highlighting their concentration and technique amidst the ongoing sounds of the match.

The United States Smash 2025 women's doubles match unfolds in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena, featuring RYU / KIM (South Korea) in light blue and black uniforms with 'POSCO' and a cartoon tiger logo, against WANG / SUN (China) in red 'CHINA' and 'LI-NING' branded uniforms. The camera alternates between wide overhead shots and close-ups of the players' focused expressions and precise movements on the 'WTT', 'JOOLA', 'GantOn', and 'GAME ON' branded court, illuminated by dynamic blue and red accents. As the rally begins, WANG / SUN (China) execute sharp, aggressive strokes, prompting a commentator to exclaim, "Oh, great backhand there by you. Jumped on top of that ball," followed by a suggestion to "watch this backhand right here." RYU / KIM (South Korea) respond with quick footwork and defensive returns. The scoreboard visually indicates WANG / SUN leading 6-0 in the current game, with the overall match score at 1-2 in their favor. Following a powerful exchange, a commentator announces "6-3!" amidst audience cheers and applause, with another noting "real power from the forehand" from WANG / SUN. The rally continues, with WANG / SUN extending their visual lead to 7-0. The two male commentators then discuss the Korean pair, with one asking, "Are they quicker, this Korean pair than the Chinese?" and the other responding, "Wouldn't say they're quicker... but they're within range." The sequence concludes with a point scored by WANG / SUN, followed by a brief reset as players prepare for the next serve.

The Maverick Arena in Las Vegas, brightly lit with 'WELCOME TO THE MAVERICK ARENA', 'LAS VEGAS', 'JOOLA', 'GantOn', and 'WTT' branding, hosts a high-intensity women's doubles table tennis match at the United States Smash 2025. The Chinese pair WANG / SUN, in red and yellow 'CHINA' uniforms, execute aggressive rallies with precise coordination, while their South Korean opponents RYU / KIM, in light blue and black 'POSCO' uniforms, display focused and agile movements. A male commentator notes the players' inherent "quickness" early on. The match is tightly contested, with the score updating from RYU / KIM 0-7 and WANG / SUN 1-4, to 1-6, and then 1-7, indicating a strong lead for the Chinese team. The camera alternates between close-ups of intense player expressions and wide shots capturing dynamic rallies, quick footwork, and powerful strokes, all punctuated by the distinct sounds of the table tennis ball hitting paddles. The umpire, seated centrally, audibly calls out scores like "Seven-five," "Seven-six," and "Seven all," and announces a "Time out" at one point. A female commentator specifically praises "Wang Manyu" for her ability to "make sure that point gets finished off quickly," noting "most are all the shots she's made in this match so far," and later comments on a "not ideal restart." The audience, visible in dim lighting, reacts with engaged cheers, roars, and applause after rallies and points, some holding signs like one for '孙颖莎' (Sun Yingsha), contributing to the electric atmosphere that matches the players' intensity.

The intense doubles table tennis match at the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena sees WANG / SUN (China) leading RYU / KIM (South Korea) 1-0 in games, with the current game tightly contested. The score progresses from 7-7 to 8-7 in favor of the Chinese pair, then 7-9, and finally 8-9. The segment opens with focused close-ups of RYU / KIM, in light blue and black 'POSCO' uniforms, as commentators note the "Korean pair" recently won two titles. The camera pulls back to reveal the dark arena floor branded with 'LAS VEGAS' and 'WTT', and an engaged audience, some holding '孙颖莎' signs, whose cheers and applause punctuate the sharp sounds of racket hits. WANG / SUN, in red 'CHINA' uniforms, execute a fast-paced rally with aggressive forehand and backhand strokes, with one commentator exclaiming, "Korea's got to stop this run by China." The umpire calls out the score "8-7" after a point, followed by "7-9" and "8-9" as the Chinese pair extends their lead. A commentator highlights a "cut" shot by "Sun" and later praises "Kim" for a "nicely done" point on the serve, winning a quick point to trail by one. Dynamic movement and precision define the play, with the vibrant environment featuring illuminated sponsor logos and a large screen briefly displaying the Statue of Liberty. The Chinese pair maintains their lead, poised for the next point amidst the enthusiastic crowd.

In a tense women's doubles table tennis match at the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena, China's WANG / SUN, dressed in red 'CHINA' and 'LI-NING' uniforms, lead South Korea's RYU / KIM, in light blue and black 'POSCO' uniforms, 1-0 in games, with the current game tied at 9-9. As Sun Y. S. wipes her face and Wang M. Y. adjusts her paddle, a male commentator's voice begins, remarking on a 'nice cut' and a 'better reaction' from a previous play. The rally commences with a serve from the Chinese team, leading to a fast-paced exchange of aggressive forehand and backhand strokes. The commentator praises the 'good shot placement' and highlights Wang Manyu's 'big arm span,' noting it was a 'fantastic effort' to get the ball past her. The Chinese players demonstrate strong coordination, with Wang M. Y. executing a powerful forehand and Sun Y. S. covering the backhand side. The rally concludes with a point for the Chinese team, bringing the score to 10-9, and the on-screen graphic updates to 'GAME POINT'. The commentator applauds a 'great decision' by one of the players (likely Sun Yingsha) for a 'non-paced ball' that 'won the point for her,' adding that 'Wang wasn't able to deal with that off-pace shot.' The audience, some holding '孙颖莎' signs, reacts with excitement, while the umpire oversees the match. The court is illuminated with JOOLA, GantOn, and WTT branding, and a large screen behind displays a cityscape with the Statue of Liberty, all contributing to the electric atmosphere of intense focus and athleticism. The commentator concludes by stating, 'Match point and all. Just calmly and smoothly.'

At the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas's Maverick Arena, a high-stakes women's doubles table tennis match unfolds between RYU/KIM (South Korea) in light blue and black, and WANG/SUN (China) in red. With the match tied 2-2 in games and WANG/SUN leading 11-10 in the second, the segment opens with an intense rally. A male commentator describes WANG/SUN winning a critical point with a "safe spinny backhand" boasting "a lot of top spin." The Chinese pair celebrate with a fist bump, while RYU/KIM show visible frustration, with the commentator imagining the Korean player Kim thinking, "What happened there?" Close-ups capture the focused expressions of players, including the Korean player with a ponytail and the Chinese player with short black hair. The commentator confirms it's game point, 11-10, for the number two seeds, noting China's "lockdown mode" and effective "safe spinny loops" while Korea is "missing both of 'em." A decisive "cut from Sun" secures the second game for WANG/SUN, eliciting a loud cheer from the dimly lit audience, some holding '孙颖莎' signs. The umpire, seated centrally, maintains a neutral demeanor amidst the prominent court branding of 'WTT', 'GantOn', 'JOOLA', and 'LAS VEGAS'. Commentators then reveal that the winners will face Wang Yidi and Kuai Man, the top seeds from China. The third game commences with "Love one," as Kim executes a "great forehand cut on the receive." The sequence concludes with the Chinese pair preparing aggressively and the Korean pair regrouping with determination, all underscored by the sounds of intense table tennis rallies and an engaged crowd.

The high-intensity doubles table tennis match at the Maverick Arena in Las Vegas, part of the United States Smash 2025, features the Chinese pair WANG / SUN in red against South Korea's RYU / KIM in light blue and black. The rally begins with WANG / SUN serving, leading to a fast-paced exchange of ball hits and agile movements. The umpire interjects with a clear 'Stop' and instructs, 'No, no flash photography. Thank you,' before the rally continues. After a point is won by WANG / SUN, met with loud crowd cheers and applause, the umpire calls 'One all.' Commentators observe the visible frustration and body language of 'Team Korea' (RYU / KIM) after missing the point, noting their 'Oh, we had that' reaction and 'near misses' in game two. The camera alternates between wide overhead shots and close-ups, highlighting the players' focused expressions and dynamic footwork. Another intense rally ensues, with commentators praising 'Sun' for an 'incredible' backhand, executed even when 'out of position,' as she manages a 'down the line' shot. The umpire then announces 'One two,' followed by another point that elicits loud crowd cheers and applause, and the umpire's call of 'Three one.' WANG / SUN celebrate with a fist bump, while RYU / KIM show visible frustration. The scoreboard updates to 2-1 in favor of WANG / SUN. The audience, seated in dimly lit stands, reacts with engagement, some holding signs, including one with '孙颖莎' (Sun Yingsha) in bold yellow characters. The umpire, seated centrally, oversees the match with a neutral demeanor, again reiterating, 'Please, no flash photography.' The court features prominent branding like 'GAME ON', 'WTT', 'JOOLA', and 'GantOn', with the Maverick Arena’s illuminated barriers displaying 'WELCOME TO THE MAVERICK ARENA' and 'LAS VEGAS'. A brief transition shows a large screen with a graphic of the Statue of Liberty and a cityscape, indicating a commercial or intermission break before the players reset for the next point, maintaining intense focus.

The segment opens with a close-up of the Chinese team, Sun Yingsha and Wang Manyu, in their red 'CHINA' and 'LI-NING' uniforms, displaying intense focus, as a commentator notes the many fans in the Maverick Arena, especially for these two players. The scene transitions to a wide-angle view of the Las Vegas arena, where the doubles match between RYU / KIM (South Korea) in light blue and black, and WANG / SUN (China) is underway. The scoreboard shows WANG / SUN leading 2-0 in games and 4-1 in the current game, a score confirmed by an audio announcement. A commentator explains a previous serve, then praises a powerful backhand by Kim, one of the Korean players. The rally begins with aggressive play from the Chinese pair, executing a fast-paced exchange that culminates in a point won by a powerful forehand from the short-haired Chinese player. A close-up reveals the short-haired Korean player, Kim, showing visible frustration. The next dynamic rally features both teams moving swiftly across the court, with the Chinese players demonstrating strong coordination and quick footwork. The Korean team responds with a sharp return, but the Chinese pair counter with precision, winning another point amidst a loud "Jia you!" chant from the audience and a commentator remarking on the speed. The scoreboard updates to 5-2 in favor of WANG / SUN, a score echoed by the commentator who highlights Wang and Sun extending their lead. The engaged audience, some holding '孙颖莎' signs, and the attentive umpire are visible before the segment concludes with a transition graphic featuring the Statue of Liberty and Las Vegas skyline, followed by commercials for Allstate and the game 'Douluo Continent'.

event_blocks.summary: At the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas, RYU / KIM (South Korea) in light blue and black faced WANG / SUN (China) in red, with the umpire calling "Fault. 6-2. Not vertical" for WANG / SUN's advantage amidst the sharp sounds of short balls. The Korean pair showed frustration, while the Chinese pair celebrated, prompting a "TTR challenge by Korea, service angle" called by the female commentator, which the male commentator clarified was legal at 25.49°, noting "Two challenges remaining" and that "these umpires are really calling it close." The match resumed with a rally, crowd noise, and a loud cheer, with the umpire announcing "6-2." The scene then shifted to a Wix advertisement featuring Noah Rosen, who explained how he ordered 200 hand-forged knives and built a Wix website to sell them, transitioning to his "Forge to Table knives" brand. Noah Rosen, founder, showcased his culinary skills, the handmade knives, and his Wix-built website, which included product pages, customer reviews, and a Forbes '30 Under 30' badge. He detailed the curated shopping experience and how Wix facilitated the precision of his website, mirroring the craftsmanship of his knives, and highlighted the recipe blog as a "creative outlet" that "provide[s] content and really good recipes" with Wix. Managing his online store's backend, he reviewed orders and sales data, including a map of US sales, and declared that his platform allowed them to "serve a thousand customers on the site every day," achieving "a million dollars in revenue," and that he was "just getting started." The segment concluded with Noah Rosen smiling, followed by a Wix advertisement screen with the tagline 'Own Your Story'.

Wix Advertisement.description: A promotional segment featuring a man in a denim shirt and glasses, showcasing his handmade Japanese-style kitchen knives under the brand 'FORGE TO TABLE'. The setting is a modern kitchen with wooden cabinetry and open shelves displaying kitchenware. The video includes close-ups of knife-making, cooking, and website interface elements such as product pages, customer reviews, and sales analytics. The man's voice explains that Wix is the platform that enabled him to build his 'online empire', providing a 'curated shopping experience' with the same 'detail and precision' as his knives, facilitating a 'creative outlet' through a recipe blog with 'really good recipes', and allowing him to serve 'a thousand customers' daily, leading to 'a million dollars in revenue'. The segment ends with a Wix advertisement screen featuring the tagline 'Own Your Story' and a call-to-action button, reinforcing Wix's role in his business success.

RYU / KIM (South Korea).description: Two female players in light blue and black uniforms; one with a ponytail and the other with short hair, both displaying focused expressions and agile movements during rallies, with visible sponsor logos like 'POSCO' and a cartoon tiger on their shirts. The player with the ponytail is seen with a determined look, while the short-haired player shows visible frustration after losing a point. They are the subject of a 'TTR challenge by Korea' regarding a service angle.

Audience.description: Spectators seated in the background, visible in dim lighting, reacting to the match with visible engagement, some leaning forward during intense rallies, and holding signs such as one with '孙颖莎' (Sun Yingsha) in bold yellow characters. Their reactions include clapping and focused observation, and their loud cheers and reactions are clearly audible during intense play.

WANG / SUN (China).description: Two female players in red uniforms with yellow trim and 'CHINA' on the back; one with short black hair and the other with short brown hair, both showing aggressive play and strong coordination, with visible 'LI-NING' and 'CHINA' branding on their shirts. They celebrate a point with a fist bump and are seen preparing for the next rally with intense focus.

Maverick Arena.description: The venue in Las Vegas, featuring a dark court with illuminated branding, including 'WELCOME TO THE MAVERICK ARENA', 'LAS VEGAS', and sponsor logos like JOOLA, GantOn, and WTT, with a large screen displaying the Statue of Liberty and cityscape during a transition. The court floor has 'GAME ON' and 'WTT' logos, and the surrounding barriers display multiple sponsor names. The arena's atmosphere is filled with the sounds of table tennis, background chatter, and crowd noise.

Umpire.description: A male official seated at the center of the court, wearing a dark shirt, overseeing the match with a neutral posture, occasionally gesturing to signal points. He remains seated and attentive throughout the rally, and his voice is heard announcing "Fault. 6-2. Not vertical" and later confirming the score as "6-2."

raw_segments: The segment opens with a women's doubles table tennis match at the United States Smash 2025 in Las Vegas, featuring RYU / KIM (South Korea) in light blue and black against WANG / SUN (China) in red. The distinct sounds of short balls punctuate the play as the umpire announces, "Fault. 6-2. Not vertical," confirming WANG / SUN's lead. Following the point, the Korean pair, RYU / KIM, walk back to their side displaying visible frustration, particularly the short-haired player, while the Chinese pair, WANG / SUN, celebrate confidently with a fist bump amidst background chatter. A female commentator notes, "I got a challenge, calling for TTR," which a male commentator clarifies as a "TTR challenge by Korea, service angle." The female commentator adds, "I mean, they may as well challenge if they've got... Quite some way off. Oh no. This is legal." The umpire reviews the service angle via TTR, confirming it was legal at 25.49°. The male commentator confirms, "So it can't be more than 30. Legal serve and worth the challenge," and adds, "Two challenges remaining," observing that "these umpires are really calling it close." The match resumes with a wide-angle view of the court under bright arena lights, accompanied by the sounds of a table tennis rally and building crowd noise, culminating in a loud cheer. The umpire then announces "6-2," and the male commentator remarks, "Hearing the crowd's reaction is all you need to know." The scene abruptly transitions to a Wix advertisement, where a man in a blue cap and plaid shirt unboxes handmade Japanese-style kitchen knives, with the website interface displayed. A male voiceover, identifying himself as Noah Rosen, states, "I ordered 200 hand-forged knives to my dorm room and built a Wix website to sell them. I'm Noah Rosen, founder..."

The video opens with an energetic, upbeat background music as a man in a denim shirt and glasses is seen in a modern, well-lit kitchen, working on a laptop. He introduces his "Forge to Table knives," explaining that after "working my way up through the culinary industry," he realized many people would "love an affordable handcrafted knife," leading him to "build my own online empire." The scene transitions to close-ups of him expertly slicing vegetables and frying an egg, showcasing his culinary skills. Detailed shots then highlight his handmade knives, showing one being sharpened and displayed in a wooden block, as he proudly holds a finished knife. The narrative shifts to his brand 'FORGE TO TABLE', with a website interface displaying product pages, customer reviews, and a Forbes '30 Under 30' badge. He states that "When someone first gets to forgetable.com, we want them to have a curated shopping experience," and emphasizes that "With Wix, the detail and precision we put into crafting every knife went into the website in the same way." The website’s functionality is demonstrated, including a recipe blog with images of delicious dishes, which he describes as their "creative outlet" where they "provide content and really good recipes" with Wix, "bringing customers into the products." The man is then seen managing his online store’s backend, reviewing orders and sales data, including a map of sales by location in the United States. He proudly declares that "Having a great platform means that we can serve a thousand customers on the site every day," achieving "a million dollars in revenue," and he's "just getting started." The segment concludes with the man smiling at the camera, followed by a Wix advertisement screen with the tagline 'Own Your Story' and a call-to-action button, as the music fades out, reinforcing the brand’s digital presence and success story powered by Wix.
